subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Per Player
- **Board** — 1 jungle board (a grid for placing tiles).
- **Jungle tiles** — 36 tiles numbered 1-36, each showing path segments (straight, curves, T-junctions, crossroads).
- **Adventurers** — 4 adventurer meeples in 4 colors (matching the 4 temples).
- **Temples** — 4 temple pieces in 4 colors.

#### Shared Components
- **Gold nuggets** — Worth 2 VP each.
- **Crystals** — Worth 1 VP each.
- **Temple treasure tokens** — Placed at temples, values 5, 4, 3, 2 (first arrival gets highest).

#### Tile Details
- Every player has an identical set of 36 tiles.
- Tiles show paths that connect edges of the tile in various configurations.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Board Setup
1. **Each player takes a board** — Place your personal jungle board in front of you.
2. **Place temples** — One player (the expedition leader) places the 4 colored temples on specific edge spaces of their board. All other players copy the exact same temple positions on their boards.
3. **Place adventurers** — The expedition leader places the 4 colored adventurers on edge spaces of the opposite side of the board from the temples. All other players copy the exact same adventurer positions.
4. **Color matching** — Each adventurer must reach the temple of the same color to score.

#### Important
- Temples go on the beach (top or right edge) and adventurers on the opposite edge (bottom or left).
- All players must have identical starting positions.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Per-Player Setup
1. **Sort tiles** — Arrange your 36 numbered tiles face up so you can find any number quickly.
2. **Board placement** — Position your board and place adventurers and temples as directed by the expedition leader.
3. **No starting resources** — Players begin with no gold or crystals.

#### Treasure Setup
- Place gold nuggets and crystals on specific tile spaces as indicated in the rules (certain path intersections on the board have printed treasure symbols).
- Place temple treasure tokens at each temple in descending order: 5 on top, then 4, 3, 2.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Expedition Leader
- **Choose one player** — This player draws tile numbers each round.
- **Simultaneous play** — Despite the leader drawing tiles, all players act simultaneously.

#### Starting State
- Each player has an empty board with 4 adventurers on one edge and 4 temples on the opposite edge.
- 36 numbered tiles ready to be placed.
- Treasures (gold and crystals) on the board.
- Temple treasure tokens stacked at each temple (5/4/3/2).

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Each Round
1. **Draw a tile** — The expedition leader randomly draws one of their remaining tiles and announces the number.
2. **All players find that tile** — Every player locates the tile with the matching number from their personal set.
3. **Choose: Place OR Discard** — Each player independently decides to either:
   - **Place the tile** on their board in any empty space (any orientation), OR
   - **Discard the tile** to move one adventurer along connected paths.

#### Simultaneous Decisions
- All players make their choice at the same time. There is no turn order.
- Players may make different choices from each other (one places, another discards).

#### 36 Rounds
- The game lasts exactly 36 rounds (one for each tile number).

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Placing a Tile
- Place the drawn tile on any empty space on your board.
- You may rotate the tile to any orientation.
- Paths do not need to connect to existing paths (but unconnected paths are useless).
- Plan paths that link adventurers to their matching temples.

#### Discarding a Tile to Move
- Discard the tile and count the number of path exits on it (2, 3, or 4).
- Move one adventurer that many spaces along connected paths on your board.
- **Straight tile (2 exits)** — Move 2 steps.
- **T-junction (3 exits)** — Move 3 steps.
- **Crossroads (4 exits)** — Move 4 steps.
- Adventurers can only move along connected, continuous path segments.
- An adventurer may not pass through another adventurer (they block the path).

#### Collecting Treasures
- If an adventurer moves onto or through a space with a gold nugget or crystal, they collect it.
- Each treasure can only be collected once (remove it from the board).

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Temple Arrival
- When an adventurer reaches their matching colored temple, they stop and claim the top temple treasure token.
- **First arrival** — Gets 5 VP token.
- **Second arrival** — Gets 4 VP token.
- **Third arrival** — Gets 3 VP token.
- **Fourth arrival** — Gets 2 VP token.
- The first player to get any adventurer to its matching temple across all players gets the highest remaining token for that color.

#### Blocking
- Adventurers block each other. You cannot move through a space occupied by another adventurer.
- Plan paths to avoid bottlenecks.

#### Path Connectivity
- Paths must physically connect from tile to tile for movement.
- Dead ends are wasted. Ensure continuous routes from adventurers to temples.

#### No Take-Backs
- Once a tile is placed or discarded, the decision is final.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Game End
- The game ends after all 36 tiles have been drawn and resolved.
- OR the game can end early if all players agree no further useful moves are possible.

#### Scoring
- **Temple treasure tokens** — VP value of tokens claimed (5, 4, 3, or 2 per temple reached).
- **Gold nuggets** — 2 VP each.
- **Crystals** — 1 VP each.
- **Unreached temples** — 0 VP for adventurers that did not reach their temple.

#### Winner
- The player with the most total VP wins.
- **Tiebreaker** — The player who reached more temples wins. If still tied, share the victory.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early Game
- **Build paths first** — In the early rounds, prioritize placing tiles to create path networks. You need connected routes before you can move.
- **Focus on 1-2 adventurers first** — Trying to build 4 complete paths simultaneously is inefficient. Create routes for your most direct adventurer-temple pairs first.
- **Treasure intersections** — Route paths through gold nuggets (2 VP) when possible. Even a slight detour for gold is worthwhile.
- **Keep tiles flexible** — In early rounds, place tiles that open up multiple routing options rather than committing to a single narrow path.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Frequent Errors
- **Discarding too early** — Discarding tiles for movement before paths are complete wastes movement potential.
- **Discarding too late** — Waiting too long to discard means adventurers cannot cover the distance to temples before tiles run out.
- **Ignoring temple race** — The difference between the 5 VP and 2 VP temple token is huge. Racing for first arrival matters.
- **Blocking your own paths** — Placing an adventurer in a bottleneck position blocks your other adventurers.
- **Forgetting tile orientation** — You can rotate tiles freely. Always check if a rotation creates a better connection.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Critical Choices
- **Place vs. discard** — The core decision every round. Place tiles when paths are incomplete; discard when routes are ready and movement is needed.
- **Which adventurer to move** — Prioritize the adventurer closest to claiming a high-value (5 or 4 VP) temple token.
- **Crossroads tiles** — 4-exit tiles give 4 movement when discarded but also create the best path intersections. Save high-exit tiles for movement in the late game.
- **Treasure collection vs. speed** — Sometimes skipping a crystal (1 VP) to reach the temple first (5 vs 2 VP = 3 VP swing) is the better play.
- **Abandoning adventurers** — Sometimes it is better to accept that one adventurer will not reach their temple and focus resources on the other three.
